inline_comments.message: Why do we need to clone this file? Can't we use the original one?
Because of line 47 and 48. Upstream uses a single IP_ADDR for SDC FE and BE, whereas we have one for FE and one for BE.

We've lost the ability to preload from robot.  There used to be a specific url for the sdnc admin gui portal now there isn't.  It should be sdnc-portal.onap-sdnc

KEYWORD BuiltIn . Log Logging in to ${SDNGC_ADMIN_LOGIN_URL}
Documentation: 	

Logs the given message with the given level.

00:28:17.702 	TRACE 	Arguments: [ 'Logging in to http://sdnhost.onap-sdnc:8843/login' ] 	
00:28:17.702 	INFO 	Logging in to http://sdnhost.onap-sdnc:8843/login 	
00:28:17.702 	TRACE 	Return: None 	
00:00:00.007 KEYWORD browser_setup . Handle Proxy Warning
00:00:00.333 KEYWORD ExtendedSelenium2Library . Title Should Be AdminPortal

I couldn't see a reason why this bumped up.  The demo manifest still says "robot_docker: 1.1-STAGING-latest"

With 1.2 I encountered a failure distributing the models:

TypeError: string indices must be integers

<kw type="for" name="${dict} IN [ @{json} ]"><kw type="foritem" name="${dict} = services"><kw name="Get From Dictionary" library="Collections"><doc>Returns a value from the given ``dictionary`` based on the given ``key``.</doc><arguments><arg>${dict}</arg><arg>uuid</arg></arguments><assign><var>${uuid}</var></assign><msg timestamp="20180126 23:11:32.925" level="TRACE">Arguments: [ 'services' | 'uuid' ]</msg><msg timestamp="20180126 23:11:32.925" level="FAIL">TypeError: string indices must be integers</msg><msg timestamp="20180126 23:11:32.925" level="DEBUG">Traceback (most recent call last):
  None</msg><status status="FAIL" endtime="20180126 23:11:32.925" starttime="20180126 23:11:32.925"/></kw><status status="FAIL" endtime="20180126 23:11:32.926" starttime="20180126 23:11:32.924"/></kw><status status="FAIL" endtime="20180126 23:11:32.926" starttime="20180126 23:11:32.924"/></kw><status status="FAIL" endtime="20180126 23:11:32.926" starttime="20180126 23:11:32.442"/></kw><status status="FAIL" endtime="20180126 23:11:32.926" starttime="20180126 23:11:32.441"/></kw>

The preload template ends up failing @ SO when deploying through VID.
I don't think its possible for an Openstack network to have the same UUID as a subnet.  The file checked into testsuite on amsterdam has this:
"onap_private_net_id" : "${GLOBAL_INJECTED_NETWORK}",
"onap_private_subnet_id" : "${GLOBAL_INJECTED_NETWORK}",

the resulting error in VID is:
"requestStatus": "Received vfModuleException from VnfAdapter: category='INTERNAL' message='Exception during create VF 400 Bad Request: The server could not comply with the request since it is either malformed or otherwise incorrect., error.type=StackValidationFailed, error.message=Property error: : resources.vdns_private_1_port.properties.fixed_ips[0].subnet: : Error validating value '247928a9-2e8c-4580-93a9-b4c719fc8404': Unable to find subnet with name or id '247928a9-2e8c-4580-93a9-b4c719fc8404'' rolledBack='true'",
"precentProgress": "100"

Please fix this upstream: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=testsuite/properties.git;a=blob;f=integration_preload_parameters.py;h=f4cc51f41054cf11882532a4805d11f024f3046c;hb=refs/heads/amsterdam

This is no longer forked.
